title: Ambiguous Vendor Comparison Report
input:
VENDOR EVALUATION — ERP SYSTEM SELECTION
Prepared by: IT Procurement Committee
Date: April 28, 2026

OBJECTIVE: Replace our legacy system (SAP R/3, deployed 2015) with a modern cloud ERP for our 3,200-employee organization across 14 countries.

EVALUATION CRITERIA (weighted):
  Functionality: 30%
  Total Cost of Ownership (5 years): 25%
  Implementation timeline: 20%
  Vendor stability & support: 15%
  User experience: 10%

─── VENDOR A — NEXUS ERP ───

Quote v3 (April 20): $4.2M for 5 years (perpetual licenses include enterprise add-ons, annual maintenance covered)
  — Originally quoted $3.8M in January. Price increased due to adding 200 additional user seats (headcount grew from 2,800 to 3,000 by Q1, then revised to 3,200 corporate + 200 contractors = 3,400 total, but they offer unlimited contractor accounts at no extra cost now)
  — Implementation: 9-12 months (original estimate was 7-9 months in January, padded because Scope B — international payroll module — was added in March)
  — Go-live target: Q3 2027

Functionality score (by evaluation team): 92/100
  Strong: Financials, Supply Chain, HR
  Weak: Advanced analytics (relying on third-party BI integration)

Support: 24/7 global, dedicated account manager included
  Support satisfaction from existing customers: 4.2/5

User experience (pilot with 50 users): 4.5/5 — praised by pilot group

Vendor stability: Public company, NASDAQ: NXUS, revenue $2.1B, growing 18% YoY

─── VENDOR B — ORACLE CLOUD ERP ───

Quote v2 (April 15): $5.8M for 5 years (SaaS subscription, includes professional services)
  — Original Q4 2025 quote was $5.2M. Price increase was due to Oracle's annual list price adjustment of ~12%. Contract negotiations brought it back down.
  — Implementation: 12-18 months (Oracle's own estimate; notably longer than Nexus)
  — Go-live target: Q1 2028

Functionality score: 95/100
  Strong: Everything, including built-in AI-powered analytics
  Weak: Pricing complexity (noted as "challenging to configure custom reports")

Support: 24/7, but some customers report 48-hour response time for non-critical issues
  Support satisfaction: 3.8/5

User experience: 3.9/5 — enterprise users find it powerful but complex

Vendor stability: Oracle, $50B+ revenue. Extremely stable.

─── VENDOR C — SAP S/4HANA CLOUD ───

Quote v1 (April 10): €4.7M (~$5.1M USD at current rates) for 5 years
  — Quote is initial and may change; SAP typically requires site visit before final pricing
  — Implementation: 10-14 months
  — Go-live: Q4 2027

Functionality score: 88/100
  Strong: Seamless upgrade path from our current R/3, Financials
  Weak: Supply Chain module less mature than competitors

Support: Standard SAP support (24/5), premium available at additional cost
  Support satisfaction: 3.6/5 (known for enterprise complexity)

User experience: 4.1/5 — familiar interface for existing SAP users

Vendor stability: SAP, existing vendor — zero migration friction for current users.

─── COMMITTEE PREFERENCE (for record):

The committee leans toward NEXUS ERP based on current quote. However, there are two concerns:
  1. The price may increase again if headcount grows beyond 3,200 — but Nexus now says contractor seats are free
  2. SAP S/4HANA has the advantage of being our existing vendor, but the quote was preliminary

Recommendation: Issue RFP addendum to SAP for confirmed pricing, then make final decision by May 30.

ground_truth.recommendation: Issue RFP addendum to SAP for confirmed pricing, then make final decision
